requirements.paper_reference: Section 4.1 Splits and protocol; Terminal-Bench-2.0 64-case evaluation set
requirements.description: Terminal-Bench-2.0 held-in and held-out split assignments are fixed across harness variants.
requirements.notes: The local toy and dry-run fixtures are not the paper's fixed 64-case live evaluation split. Bundle verification applies cross_artifact_split_evaluation_coverage so repeat-evaluation task ids must equal this fixed split, cross_artifact_capture_run_id_binding so primary live evidence belongs to one capture run, and cross_artifact_harbor_version_binding so split evidence must match the bundled live Harbor preflight environment.

requirements.paper_reference: Section 4.1 Metrics; two repeated attempts per harness candidate unless otherwise specified
requirements.description: Live evaluation artifacts show two repeated attempts per task under the fixed candidate harness.
requirements.notes: Algorithmic evaluation repeats are implemented locally; this row tracks live Terminal-Bench repeat evidence. The artifact carries aggregate task_count, attempt_count, pass_count, and fail_count values that must reconcile with per_task_attempts. Bundle verification applies cross_artifact_split_evaluation_coverage so this report must cover the same 64 task ids as the fixed split manifest, cross_artifact_capture_run_id_binding so it belongs to the same live capture run, and cross_artifact_protocol_binding so it must reference the bundled fixed protocol config hash.

requirements.paper_reference: Section 4.2 Main Results; model backend, tool set, budget, benchmark environment, and evaluator fixed
requirements.description: The live reproduction run records a fixed model, evaluator, tool set, decoding/tool budget, Self-Harness round count, and proposal width for all harness variants.
requirements.notes: P50 tracks the paper's three evaluated model backends explicitly; Anthropic remains a package adapter seam but is not a paper backend. The fixed protocol also carries the paper's Self-Harness rounds T and proposal width K. Bundle verification applies cross_artifact_capture_run_id_binding so the fixed protocol declaration belongs to the same live capture run as the primary evidence, and cross_artifact_proposer_round_count so proposer logs must match T and K.

requirements.paper_reference: Section 3.2 Harness Proposal and Section 4.2 Main Results; proposals are generated by the evaluated base models
requirements.description: The live reproduction run records proposer-side LLM request and response hashes for the paper model backends used during harness proposal.
requirements.notes: Model preflight proves paper backend reachability; this artifact binds the captured proposer traffic to the same paper backend set without storing raw prompts or responses in the reproduction bundle. Bundle verification applies cross_artifact_capture_run_id_binding and cross_artifact_proposer_model_binding so proposer-observed backends must match the fixed protocol and model preflight evidence.

requirements.paper_reference: Section 3.3 Harness Proposal; bounded proposal context includes editable surfaces, held-in failure patterns, passing behavior summaries, and previous attempted edits
requirements.description: The live reproduction run records compact hashes and counts proving the proposer context carried the paper-required context ingredients for each Self-Harness round.
requirements.notes: This artifact binds proposer request traffic to the four paper-required bounded context ingredients without storing full raw prompts, traces, or responses in the reproduction bundle. Bundle verification applies cross_artifact_capture_run_id_binding and cross_artifact_proposer_context_binding so context rounds align with proposer LLM rounds, fixed protocol Self-Harness rounds, non-empty ingredient evidence whenever proposals are attempted, and pairwise-distinct editable-surface sha256 values within each context round. It also applies cross_artifact_proposer_previous_edits_binding so previous attempted edits bind to prior proposer rounds, targeted mechanism hashes, optional causal_status_sha256 values, edited surface hashes, audit decisions, and pairwise-distinct (proposal_round_index, targeted_mechanism_sha256, edited_surface_sha256) signatures within each context round, plus cross_artifact_proposer_context_evidence_binding so each context round's held-in failure pattern task_ids cover exactly that round's baseline held-in failing task set, optional failure_category values match disclosed baseline task failure categories, optional shared_symptoms_sha256 and verifier_evidence_sha256 values are validated and recorded for paper Section 3.2 cluster evidence, optional presentation_order and actionability_hint_sha256 values are validated and recorded for the paper cluster-ordering contract, and each passing behavior summary covers exactly that round's baseline held-in passing task set as disclosed by proposal_validation_manifest.baseline_split_outcomes.task_outcomes, with passing summary task_id_set_sha256 strictly recomputed from those task_ids. Held-in failure patterns in the same round must carry distinct (failure_category, causal_status_sha256, mechanism_sha256) signatures and pairwise-disjoint task_ids so exact-match paper clusters cannot duplicate the same failure signature or assign one failing task to multiple clusters. When presentation_order is declared, it must form a contiguous permutation and larger size values must precede smaller size values; equal-size ties may be ordered by actionability. Capture-manifest diffing also emits proposer-context-evidence-derivation to compare realized proposer context task coverage, editable-surface duplicate counts, previous-attempted-edit duplicate counts, failure-pattern task-overlap counts, failure categories, causal-status hashes, shared-symptom hashes, verifier-evidence hashes, presentation order, and actionability-hint hashes against the planned held-in split. support_rank is not stored.

requirements.paper_reference: Section 3.4 Proposal Validation; candidate harnesses are evaluated, accepted, rejected, merged, or invalidated against the fixed validation protocol
requirements.description: The live reproduction run records per-round proposal validation decisions, changed surfaces, repeat metadata, split outcomes, committed candidates, structured invalid/rejected/superseded reasons, and proposer-context grounding for current candidates.
requirements.notes: This artifact is derived from the audit directory after capture, not authored by the proposer. Bundle verification applies cross_artifact_capture_run_id_binding and cross_artifact_proposal_validation_binding so validation rounds bind to the fixed protocol hash, Self-Harness round count, proposal width, proposer attempted/committed counts, proposer request/response hashes when declared, two-repeat evaluation metadata, proposer-context current candidate grounding, proposer-context previous attempted edits, canonical live split totals, proposal-validation task outcomes when present, Algorithm 1 split-outcome lineage continuity for no-op, single-commit, and declared multi-commit transitions with merged split-outcome evidence, optional harness-state hash continuity for no-op, single-commit, and declared multi-commit transitions, and the Section 3.4 acceptance rule. Within each round, candidate split_outcomes.evaluation_repeats must match baseline_split_outcomes.evaluation_repeats before aggregate pass-count validation is trusted. cross_artifact_proposer_context_evidence_binding also requires same-round baseline task_outcomes when proposer context is bundled, because weakness mining at round t must be derived from the current harness h_t rather than the final post-commit evaluation. Current candidates must target same-round proposer-context failure mechanisms, candidates with changed surfaces must bind to same-round editable surface hashes and names, and candidate mechanism/surface signatures must be distinct within each round. Accepted and merged candidates must improve at least one split, degrade neither split versus their round baseline, and target pairwise-distinct editable surface hashes within the round before the paper Algorithm 1 MERGEACCEPTED compatibility step is trusted. It records changed surfaces plus non-empty rejection reasons for rejected, superseded, and invalid candidates. Invalid candidates also carry validation_failure_category, closed to no_editable_surface or execution_failure; no_editable_surface is the only candidate category allowed to have empty changed_surfaces. Every non-no_editable_surface candidate must declare exactly one changed surface. Optional task_outcomes disclose task-level split/pass evidence and must reconcile with aggregate split counts; failing task rows may carry closed terminal failure_category values, which bind proposer-context failure patterns back to same-round baseline terminal causes. Optional paired harness_before_sha256 and harness_after_sha256 fields bind validation evidence to audit lineage harness states when extraction can derive them; legacy manifests may omit both. Multi-commit rounds that declare harness hashes also declare harness_after_merged_sha256, derived from the same lineage harness_after_hash, so the next round can bind to the merged harness state. They also declare merged_split_outcomes, derived from proposal_id __merge__ candidate audit rows, so the next baseline can bind to an independent merged-harness split observation. Optional proposer_round_request_sha256 and proposer_round_response_sha256 bind validation evidence to the shaped proposer_llm_request_log without storing raw prompts or responses. Capture-manifest diffing compares single-surface violation counts, harness-hash presence counts, multi-commit merged-hash values, merged split-outcome presence and digest, candidate changed-surface names, accepted/merged surface hashes, and task-outcome digest v2 content so rehearsal plans catch multi-surface, surface-name, MERGEACCEPTED surface-set, lineage-hash-presence, merged-hash, merged split-outcome, and terminal-category drift. Validation pass counts are not compared with the final post-commit two-repeat evaluation because baseline and per-candidate rows describe different harness states.

requirements.paper_reference: Appendix A.1 Terminal-Bench-2.0 configuration; Harbor execution environment
requirements.description: Terminal-Bench tasks execute through a provisioned Harbor environment rather than dry-run replay.
requirements.notes: Dry-run Harbor command construction does not satisfy this live execution requirement. Bundle verification applies cross_artifact_capture_run_id_binding and cross_artifact_harbor_version_binding so the live Harbor preflight version and capture run must match the bundled fixed split evidence.

requirements.paper_reference: Appendix A.1 Terminal-Bench-2.0 configuration; containerized terminal tasks
requirements.description: Docker/container execution is available and the trusted container image policy is satisfied by digest-bound evidence.
requirements.notes: Offline image-policy and scanner fixtures are release evidence, not live container runtime evidence. Bundle verification applies cross_artifact_capture_run_id_binding so image trust evidence must belong to the same live capture run as the other primary artifacts. When live Harbor audit rows record image_digest, cross_artifact_audit_image_binding also requires the executed audit image digest set to match this trust report. For multi-arch Harbor manifests, trust report child_digests bind executed audit digests to per-platform children rather than parent manifest digests.

requirements.description: MiniMax M2.5 is reachable under operator-owned hosted API credentials for the live reproduction run.
requirements.notes: This row is paper-backend-specific; it must not be satisfied by the Anthropic reference adapter. Bundle verification applies cross_artifact_capture_run_id_binding so model preflight evidence must belong to the same live capture run as the other primary artifacts.

requirements.description: Qwen3.5-35B-A3B is provisioned behind an operator-owned local deployment endpoint for the live reproduction run.
requirements.notes: The paper used a local Qwen deployment; this row tracks deployment readiness rather than hosted API credentials. Bundle verification applies cross_artifact_capture_run_id_binding so model preflight evidence must belong to the same live capture run as the other primary artifacts.

requirements.description: GLM-5.2 is reachable under operator-owned Z.ai credentials for the live reproduction run.
requirements.notes: This row is paper-backend-specific; local GLM architect access is not benchmark reproduction evidence. Bundle verification applies cross_artifact_capture_run_id_binding so model preflight evidence must belong to the same live capture run as the other primary artifacts.

requirements.paper_reference: Appendix A.1 Terminal-Bench-2.0 configuration; outbound cap and mirrored stable resources
requirements.description: Live execution records the controlled network/resource policy used for Terminal-Bench tasks.
requirements.notes: P49 binds this to Harbor readiness until live operator attestations are available. Bundle verification applies cross_artifact_capture_run_id_binding so network controls evidence must belong to the same live capture run as the other primary artifacts.

requirements.paper_reference: Section 3.4 validation and Appendix A.1; verifier outcome over final container state
requirements.description: Live Harbor trial artifacts are captured and ingested into audit-compatible evidence.
requirements.notes: Artifact ingest exists locally, but this row requires artifacts from a live run. Bundle verification applies cross_artifact_audit_split_coverage so live Harbor audit task ids must match the fixed split and two-repeat evaluation task ids with two captured attempts per task, cross_artifact_capture_run_id_binding so the audit belongs to the same live capture run as the other primary artifacts, cross_artifact_protocol_binding so the audit must reference the bundled fixed protocol config hash, and cross_artifact_audit_image_binding when audit rows carry image_digest. Multi-arch Harbor evidence binds audit image_digest values to trust-report child_digests when those are declared.

requirements.paper_reference: Section 4.1 Splits and protocol; held-out split never shown to proposer
requirements.description: Audit verification confirms held-out traces are not exposed to the proposer evidence bundle.
requirements.notes: Local invariant tests enforce this offline; a live reproduction claim also needs the live Harbor dependency provisioned.

requirements.paper_reference: Self-Harness auditability requirement; recorded, testable, reversible changes
requirements.description: Release/operator evidence binds package artifacts, provenance, attestation material, and reproduction-readiness results.
requirements.notes: The default release-candidate path remains non-reproduction; this row tracks the stricter reproduction-candidate evidence bundle.
